refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: timely updating of the worksite model according to customer requirements. It does not combine multiple functions or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target (e.g., ≤ 1.0 s).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.

candidate_dps: Incremental update mechanism that processes only changed data to minimize latency
Batch update mechanism that refreshes the entire worksite model at fixed intervals
Event-driven update mechanism triggered by detected changes in the worksite environment
Parallel processing of update tasks to meet timeliness requirements
Prioritization of critical update elements to ensure essential data is refreshed within latency constraints
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incremental update mechanism that processes only changed data to minimize latency

potential_sources: Integration with data sources providing update events
coupling_check.notes: The incremental update mechanism logically isolates update processing to only changed data, reducing coupling with full model refresh processes and supporting independence of update tasks.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and timely detection of changes in the worksite environment is available
Incremental updates can be applied without causing inconsistencies in the worksite model
Latency target of p95 ≤ 1.0 s is achievable with incremental processing given system resources
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the change detection mechanism as a separate DP to further isolate responsibilities and reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core data processing and update mechanisms for the system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives data updates and sends processed incremental updates.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles communication of incremental updates to other system components.

module_containment_scores.notes: The incremental update mechanism logically fits within NGHE Solution as it handles data processing; minimal diffusion expected outside this module.
Vehicle module may use updates but is more focused on physical control; containment is weak and diffusion high.
Teleoperation relies on processed data but does not implement update mechanisms; poor containment.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety logic, not data update processing; very weak containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The incremental update mechanism is best contained within the NGHE Solution module, which is responsible for core data processing and update logic, minimizing diffusion and maintaining cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Telemetry and Network Link may cause some coupling outside NGHE Solution.
If incremental updates require real-time safety checks, some diffusion to Safety Services might occur.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of an incremental update mechanism that processes only changed data directly addresses the FR of updating the worksite model within the required timeliness by minimizing latency. This logical solution concept aligns well with the functional requirement and supports meeting the latency acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Telemetry and Network Link may cause some coupling outside NGHE Solution.
If incremental updates require real-time safety checks, some diffusion to Safety Services might occur.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The incremental update mechanism is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has a high containment score (8) and low diffusion score (2). Other modules show weak containment and higher diffusion, supporting the hypothesis that NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary. Minor diffusion risks exist due to external interfaces and possible safety-related interactions but are currently minimal.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring the update latency (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) in the NGHE Solution module during supported update scenarios, leveraging the Telemetry and Network Link interfaces for data flow.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate NGHE Solution clearly encapsulates the incremental update mechanism, providing a clear allocation of responsibility and architectural packaging for the FR realization.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface contracts with Telemetry and Network Link to manage coupling and minimize diffusion risks.
Assess and clarify any real-time safety check requirements that might necessitate interaction with Safety Services to avoid unintended diffusion.
Include performance monitoring within NGHE Solution to verify latency acceptance criteria during operation.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of functional decomposition adequately addresses the functional requirement with a suitable design parameter and implementation module. The incremental update mechanism is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, with manageable diffusion risks and feasible verification methods. Given the high safety criticality, the recommended revisions focus on documentation and monitoring rather than further decomposition. Therefore, no further sub-FRs derivation is necessary at this stage.

revise.why: To explicitly document interface contracts with Telemetry and Network Link to manage coupling and minimize diffusion risks, clarify real-time safety check requirements to avoid unintended diffusion, and include performance monitoring within NGHE Solution to verify latency acceptance criteria during operation.

assumptions: The incremental update mechanism sufficiently meets the latency and safety requirements at the current level of decomposition.
NGHE Solution module ownership and containment are clearly defined and stable.
Interface complexity and diffusion risks are minimal and manageable with documentation and monitoring.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity with Telemetry and Network Link causing coupling outside NGHE Solution.
Possible diffusion to Safety Services if real-time safety checks are required.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface contracts with Telemetry and Network Link.
Clarification of real-time safety check requirements and their impact on module diffusion.
Performance monitoring data demonstrating update latency (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) during operation.
